title: terminalia myrtifolia
intro: Terminalia myrtifolia, also known as Neem of the gods or Jivanti, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to India and Sri Lanka, belonging to the Combretaceae family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Terminalia myrtifolia include the treatment of fever, cough, and digestive issues, as well as its ability to boost the immune system and reduce stress. Terminalia myrtifolia can be prepared into herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
